title: Undercut pressure proxy from position-neighborhood context
summary: Official F1 strategy explanations emphasize undercut, overcut, and go-long decisions, with the following car often needing to be within striking range.
rationale: Official F1 strategy explanations emphasize undercut, overcut, and go-long decisions, with the following car often needing to be within striking range. The dataset lacks gaps, but Position, LapTime_Delta, relative pace, and recent nearby pit events can proxy whether a driver is vulnerable to being undercut or likely to respond next lap.
implementation_hint: For each race-lap, create features comparing a driver to adjacent positions: previous/current lap PitStop of cars at Position-1 and Position+1, relative LapTime and LapTime_Delta versus position band median, lost_position_recently, behind_car_pitted_recently, ahead_car_pitted_recently, and interaction with track/race and tyre age.
expected_effect: Adds strategic opponent pressure not captured by tyre degradation alone; may help distinguish drivers with similar tyre life but different tactical urgency.
risk: Without true time gaps, position-neighborhood proxies are noisy. Needs strict same-lap leave-one-out handling and grouped validation to avoid memorizing race templates.
